name: Valdepeñas de Jaen – via La Pandera
name_original: Valdepeñas de Jaen – por La Pandera
region_intro: Road cycling around Valdepeñas De Jaén is characterized by its diverse and challenging terrain within the Sierra Sur de Jaén mountain range. The region features significant elevation changes, including the iconic Sierra de la Pandera climb, and extensive olive groves that create a distinct landscape. This area offers a blend of rugged mountain passes and rolling hills, providing varied environments for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Valdepeñas De Jaén The most popular road cycling route is Valdepeñas de Jaen – via La Pandera, a 17.8 miles (28.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 38 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes the Sierra de la Pandera, a climb featured multiple times in the Vuelta a España. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is El Oasis 2 – El Curvin loop from Cárcheles, a moderate 38.7 miles (62.3 km) path. This loop offers varied terrain and scenic views typical of the Sierra Sur de Jaén. Local road cyclists also love the Río Frío Spring – Castle of Santa Catalina loop from Valdepeñas de Jaén, a 41.6 miles (67.0 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 58 minutes. Road cycling around Valdepeñas De Jaén is defined by challenging mountain passes, extensive olive groves, and the rugged peaks of the Sierra Sur de Jaén. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Valdepeñas De Jaén are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 3 reviews. More than 700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Valdepeñas De Jaén's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Valdepeñas De Jaén?
A: Road cycling in Valdepeñas De Jaén offers a diverse and challenging terrain, primarily within the Sierra Sur de Jaén mountain range. You'll encounter significant elevation changes, rugged mountain passes, and extensive olive groves that create a unique 'sea of olives' landscape. The area features both demanding climbs, like the iconic Sierra de la Pandera, and rolling hills.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging road cycling climbs in the area?
A: Yes, the region is known for challenging climbs, most notably the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996248118/valdepenas-de-jaen-via-la-pandera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Pandera&lt;/a&gt;. This ascent has been featured multiple times in the Vuelta a España, offering a substantial elevation gain and steep gradients, with panoramic views from the summit.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning views of the 'sea of olives' landscape and the rugged peaks of the Sierra Sur de Jaén. You might also encounter natural spots like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3230098" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Tajos&lt;/a&gt; gorge or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4381553" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Frío Spring&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1598600" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Quiebrajano Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is another notable viewpoint.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, Valdepeñas De Jaén offers several longer routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40365173" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Embalse del Víboras – Viboras Reservoir loop from Los Villares&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route spanning over 90 km, providing a substantial challenge for long-distance cyclists.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Valdepeñas De Jaén?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the challenging mountain passes, the extensive olive groves, and the varied terrain that provides options for all ability levels.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Valdepeñas De Jaén?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1286047" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Castillo – View of Alcalá la Real loop from Valdepeñas de Jaén&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a challenging ride through varied landscapes.

Q: How many road cycling routes are there in Valdepeñas De Jaén?
A: There are over 40 road cycling routes available around Valdepeñas De Jaén, catering to various difficulty levels from moderate to difficult.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Valdepeñas De Jaén?
A: While the guide doesn't specify an exact best season, mountain regions like the Sierra Sur de Jaén are generally ideal for cycling in spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be hot, especially in the lower olive groves, while winters might bring colder conditions to higher elevations.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by significant historical or cultural points of interest?
A: While the focus is on natural beauty and challenging terrain, some routes might offer glimpses of local settlements or historical sites. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31473997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Frío Spring – Castle of Santa Catalina loop from Valdepeñas de Jaén&lt;/a&gt; passes through varied landscapes that may include views of historical elements like the Castle of Santa Catalina.

Q: What are some moderate difficulty road cycling options in the region?
A: For those looking for a moderate challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24528673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Oasis 2 – El Curvin loop from Cárcheles&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice. This route offers varied terrain and scenic views without the extreme gradients of the most difficult climbs.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of reservoirs or bodies of water?
A: Yes, some routes provide scenic views of local reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40365173" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Embalse del Víboras – Viboras Reservoir loop from Los Villares&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering expansive vistas of the Viboras Reservoir.
tips: Tough climb. From Valdepeñas de Jaen we find ourselves with 13.17km at 6.8% of average slope but with several kms above 10%. Its top is located at 1,809 meters above sea level, accumulating a drop of 906 meters.
You can drive up to the Geodesic vertex. But it is recommended to do some route.

Parish Church dated between the 16th and 17th centuries. Its main promoter was the architect and sculptor Juan de Reolid in 1539. During the Spanish Civil War the temple suffered significant damage, being restored and reformed by the architect Ramón Pajares Pardo in 1952.
